svg.title: Gray-Scott horizon comparison
svg.desc: The same feed-vs-kill grid at 700 and 1400 steps. This is the cutoff check: which cells were already real, and which ones were still moving?
svg.text: The same feed-vs-kill grid at 700 and 1400 steps. This is the cutoff check: which cells were already real, and which ones were still moving?

text.tspan: The early map: what still looks chemically busy before the longer run settles.
Positive = more occupied later. Negative = early overread.
The later map: the same grid after the longer chemical horizon.
Positive = sharper later fronts. Negative = smoothing or collapse.
Biggest active growth: F=0.022, k=0.051, Δactive=+0.321.
Sharpest late front growth: F=0.022, k=0.051, Δedge=+0.0170.
This is the practical read: the coarse map was useful, but not every bright cell had finished becoming itself.
Some settings keep growing into clearer bands, while others looked active early and then cooled almost all the way out.
